intro: Loxanisa gracillima, also known as the "Mexican Lophostemum," is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Loxanisa gracillima include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Loxanisa gracillima is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
